description_long: This is a two-story brick house with a prominent front porch supported by white columns and a decorative railing. The facade features a mix of red brick and white trim, with a gabled roof section above the porch and a flat roof covering the main structure. The windows are symmetrically arranged on the front, with multi-pane upper sashes and decorative white framing. The sides of the house are simpler, with evenly spaced rectangular windows and minimal ornamentation. Two brick chimneys rise from the flat roof, and the rear of the house includes a secondary porch with steps. The overall design suggests a blend of early 20th-century American residential styles, with Craftsman and Colonial Revival influences.
description: A two-story red brick house with a front porch, white trim, and a flat main roof with two chimneys. The facade is detailed with symmetrical windows and decorative elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entry porch with white columns, decorative railing, and symmetrical windows with white trim.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the long brick wall with evenly spaced windows and two chimneys on the flat roof.
description_view_2: Rear view featuring a secondary porch, simple brickwork, and a set of steps leading to the back door.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a similar window arrangement and visible porch structure, highlighting the house's rectangular footprint.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows with multi-pane upper sashes
attribute_entryway_design: Covered front porch with white columns, decorative railing, brick steps, and a single main door
